CFPB COMPLAINT Product : CITI XXXX Credit Card Company : Citibank , N.A . 
Issue : Improper account closure ; inaccurate credit reporting ; failure to honor notice ; failure to resolve dispute WHAT HAPPENED In XXXX of XXXX, Citibank sent me a written notice that my credit card account ending in XXXX would be closed due to inactivity on XX/XX/2025 unless I made a qualifying transaction before that date. 
To keep the account open, I made a purchase on XX/XX/2025, prior to account closure of the stated deadline. Despite my compliance with the notice, Citibank closed the account anyway on XX/XX/2025. 
After noticing the unexpected closure, I contacted Citibank ( date unknown ) and informed them that I had made a qualifying transaction before the deadline. I questioned how my account could be considered inactive when a valid transaction had occurred. 
Citibank representatives acknowledged the closure but refused to reopen the account or correct it, stating that nothing can be done and that I must pay the balance and accept the consequences. No one provided a clear explanation for why the account was closed contrary to the written notice. 
Following the closure, my automatic payment failed because the account had been shut down by Citis mistake. This caused my account to become delinquent, which I did not consent to and which would not have occurred had Citi properly honored the terms of their own notice. 
I contacted Citibank again on Saturday, XX/XX/XXXX, and the representative again claimed there was nothing they could do, despite the fact that the account was closed in error. Over 100 days, Citibank has failed to investigate, failed to correct the issue, and failed to provide any resolution. I also requested All Statements, Missive, Memos, and Notices. I was provided with partial paperwork, statements only, over the allotted 10 day timeline I was given. 

HOW THIS HARMED ME Citibanks improper closure : -Incorrectly triggered delinquency due to their wrongful account c

Chase sent me an automated fraud alert for an unfamiliar {$5.00} charge. I responded No, expecting the bank to verify the transaction. Instead, Chase immediately shut down my debit card without providing any notice that the card was being deactivated. I learned of the shutdown only after multiple legitimate recurring payments began failing.

Why this is a violation : Chases actions appear to violate several consumer-protection standards : Regulation E ( 12 CFR 1005 ) requires that banks provide timely notice when restricting access to a consumers debit card or electronic fund transfers. Chase did not notify me in any way that the card was being closed.

This is potentially an unfair practice under UDAAP because Chases automated decision caused significant and predictable harm ( failed payments, fees, service interruptions ) while offering no reasonable consumer benefit and providing no opportunity for me to correct or verify the alert.

The card closure was disproportionate to the situationone low-dollar charge with no fraud patternshowing a systemically unreasonable fraud-workflow design that harms consumers.

No adverse action notice was provided, despite restricting my access to an active payment instrument linked to multiple accounts.

Chase failed to follow reasonable verification procedures before taking action that impacted every account linked to that debit card.

Resulting damages : Because Chase closed the card without notice, several legitimate payments were declined, causing financial loss, service interruptions, and the risk of negative credit reporting. Chases conduct deprived me of access to an active and relied-upon payment method with no opportunity to prevent the harm.

What I am requesting : The CFPB should escalate this matter to Chase and require the bank to : Provide a written explanation of their justification for closing a debit card without any notice after a routine fraud-alert response.

Explain how this practice complies with Regulation E

To Whom It May Concern at the CFPB, I am submitting this complaint regarding Cash Apps failure to resolve a fraudulent transaction I reported. 

On XX/XX/year>, I filed a dispute with Cash App concerning a fraudulent transaction. 
As of XX/XX/year>, it has been 94 days since my initial complaint, far exceeding the XXXX-day investigation period Cash App advised. 
Despite repeated emails and chats with Cash App XXXX, I have only been told the matter was escalated, with no clear explanation of the investigation or progress. 


Most concerning is the inconsistency in Cash Apps handling of the case : Cash App refunded {$13.00} of the fraudulent transaction. 
However, the remaining {$660.00} has not been refunded, despite being part of the same fraudulent transaction. 
Cash App has provided no explanation for why XXXX portion was refunded while the other remains unresolved. 


This prolonged delay, lack of transparency, and inconsistent resolution are unacceptable. I have provided all necessary information multiple times, yet Cash App continues to stall without justification. 

I respectfully request that the CFPB intervene to ensure Cash App completes its investigation and issues the full refund owed to me. I am prepared to provide copies of all correspondence with Cash App to demonstrate the repeated delays and lack of resolution. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

Transaction ID : XXXX My Cash App username : XXXX A {$550.00} payment was sent to a Cash App account tied to an old phone number ( Recipient Account : XXXX ), for services already rendered by the intended recipient. The intended recipient can not access that account. I, the sender, requested a refund using the in app option. Cash App rejected that request even though the recipient account was dormant. A dormant account with no activity should automatically trigger a return of funds under any reasonable standard. I then filed a dispute through the app. I selected the exact, correct dispute reason which states that the recipient can not access the account. Instead of resolving the dispute, Cash App issued an irrelevant denial claiming that my account was not compromised. That was never the issue. This response ignored the dispute reason I selected and pretended I chose something else. This is deceptive and appears designed to trap funds in inaccessible accounts. Additionally, the denial took several days, even though it was clear that no human looked at it, indicating that Cash App also purposefully slows automated resolutions for several days, where the outcome is a denial, to make the appear

sample_narratives:
Subject : Capital One closed both of my credit card accounts with no notice and refuses to disclose the reason. 

Complaint : Capital One abruptly closed both of my credit card accounts without any prior notice, written correspondence, or adverse action letter. I discovered the closure while traveling in XXXX when my card was declined. I initially assumed the issue was related to travel, but it was not. 

I immediately contacted Capital One multiple times by phone and in person at a Capital One XXXX location. My case was escalated several times, but no one provided any explanation for the closures. I was repeatedly told they could not see the reason or could not share it. This violates the Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( Regulation B ), which requires a creditor to provide a clear, specific reason for account closure within 30 days. 

My accounts were in good standing. I have never missed payments and I regularly paid my balances in full. At the time of closure my balance was higher than normal due to international travel, but still current and paid on time. 

I received no written notice, no adverse action explanation, and no follow-up communication of any kind. Capital One still expects me to pay the remaining balance, yet refuses to explain why the account was closed or provide the adverse action notice required by law. 

I am requesting CFPB assistance to : Require Capital One to provide the legally-required adverse action notice and the specific reason for closing both accounts. 

Confirm that no negative information was reported to credit bureaus. 

Ensure I am not penalized with additional fees or interest due to their improper closure. 

Require Capital One to provide full account documentation, including internal notes, reason codes, and any fraud/risk flags that led to the closure. 

Determine whether the accounts can be reinstated, since they were closed without cause and without required notice. 

I have made every good faith effort to resolve this with C

Capital One closed my brand-new secured credit card account immediately after opening and is now refusing to refund my {$200.00} security deposit within a reasonable timeframe. They told me I must wait XXXX billing cycles, which is an excessive and unlawful delay. 

What happened : I opened a Capital One secured credit card and paid the required {$200.00} security deposit. The account was brand new and had no activity on it. Shortly after opening, Capital One placed the account on hold and then closed it without providing a clear or specific reason. 

When I contacted Capital One, they stated that my {$200.00} security deposit would not be refunded for XXXX billing cycles. This is unacceptable and in violation of multiple federal consumer protection laws. 

The account was never used, there is no balance owed, and Capital One has completed whatever internal review they claim to have. There is no reason for this delay. 

Laws potentially violated : 1. 12 CFR 1026.12 ( d ) ( 2 ) Regulation Z ( Refund of Security Deposits ) The law requires the issuer to refund the security deposit promptly after the account is closed. Capital Ones policy of holding my money for XXXX billing cycles is not prompt and appears to violate this requirement.

2. 15 U.S.C. 1691 ( d ) & 12 CFR 1002.9 ECOA / Regulation B ( Adverse Action Notice ) Capital One closed my account but did not provide a proper written adverse action notice explaining the reason for closure, which is required under federal law.

3. 12 U.S.C. 5531 UDAAP ( Unfair, Deceptive, Abusive Acts and Practices ) Holding a customers security deposit for 60 days, without justification and without clear communication, constitutes unfair and abusive financial practices. 

Harm : Capital One is holding {$200.00} of my money with no legal justification. I am experiencing financial hardship, and their refusal to refund the deposit promptly is causing real harm. I can not access funds that legally belong to me.

In early XX/XX/2025, my Capital One secured credit card account was mishandled in a way that caused significant financial and personal harm. I received an email from Capital One stating that one or more phone numbers on my profile had been changed, even though I did not authorize or request any change. Concerned about unauthorized access, I immediately contacted customer service. 

During my call, instead of assisting me or securing my account, the representative closed my account without my permission. I never received the card, never activated it, and never had the opportunity to use the account. This closure was done improperly and without my consent.

I spent over 4 hours on multiple calls with several agents, supervisors, and managers trying to understand why my account was closed and where my security deposit went. No one provided a clear explanation, no one helped resolve the issue, and in one call I was told that my deposit might not be refunded.

Because of the length of these calls caused by Capital Ones handling errors, I also lost my job that day. This has caused serious financial damage.

After being told by Capital One to apply for a new account, I was denied. The denial was based on a negative XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX report. My credit score is XXXX, which is excellent, so the denial was not related to my creditworthiness. Instead, it resulted from Capital Ones improper internal reporting after they wrongfully closed my account and created a risk flag.

This entire situation the unauthorized profile change, the improper account closure, the withholding of my deposit, the failure to assist me, the damage to my XXXX  record, and the loss of my job is the direct result of Capital Ones negligence.

I am requesting CFPB assistance to correct this situation and prevent further harm.

Chase has mishandled the situation, failed to process a proper fraud claim, closed my account without documentation, and continues to withhold my money without justification. 

I lost my debit card, which unfortunately had the PIN written on the back. An unauthorized individual used my card information to attempt fraud by trying to deposit a {$17000.00} check with my personal details written on it. 
I want to be clear : I did not deposit this check I did not authorize any such transaction I never touched or withdrew any funds related to this attempted fraud I contacted Chase multiple times and requested to file a fraud claim, but I was transferred repeatedly and told no one could assist me. Chase closed my account and only sent a final balance statement no account-closure letter, no fraud documentation, nothing explaining the decision. 

I was originally told that my remaining balance of {$900.00} would be mailed to me within XXXX business days. 
However, when I called for an update on XX/XX/year>, I was told : My funds would not be released The delay was due to an open investigation involving the fraudulent check I would need to wait indefinitely until the check issuers bank finished its review Chase could not provide a timeline or any written explanation The fraudulent {$17000.00} check has nothing to do with my personal funds. There is no legal basis for Chase to hold my {$900.00}. I have been left without access to my own money, with no updates, documentatio

On XX/XX/XXXX, multiple fraudulent transactions were attempted on my account from the same merchant ( XXXX, TN ). Several attempts were declined by Bank of America, which proves these were unauthorized and part of a fraud pattern. If I had authorized one of these charges, I would have authorized all of them but I did not authorize ANY of the transactions. The system flagged the others as fraud, yet Bank of America incorrectly concluded that the {$500.00} transaction was approved by me. This is inconsistent and violates Regulation Es requirement for a reasonable investigation.Bank of America denied my initial claim and then denied my appeal, stating that the transaction was authorized by me or someone with permission to use my card. This is incorrect. No one else has permission to use my card or account, and I did not authorize this transaction.

On XX/XX/XXXX, I deposited {$1900.00} cash at a Chase ATM. The ATM printed only a small partial slip, so I immediately contacted Chase. They provided a temporary credit of {$1900.00}, and I believed the issue was resolved. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, Chase reversed the temporary credit and withdrew the full {$1900.00} from my account. 

I contacted Chase again on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX to ask why the claim was denied, since I had the deposit slip. I was told to submit the slip, which I did. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, Chase issued a partial credit of {$580.00}, stating that only {$580.00} was found and the remaining {$1300.00} was not found and the investigation was completed. 

I called Chase again on XXXX and XXXX requesting a proper investigation because : In the first investigation, Chase claimed {$0.00} was found. 
In the second investigation, they claimed {$580.00} was found. 
These inconsistent results indicate the investigation was not done properly. 

I asked multiple agents what steps were taken in the investigation and whether the ATM, vault, deposit logs, and surveillance were reviewed. No one provided any details. I was repeatedly told, without explanation, that the case was closed.

Navy Federal failed to disclose specific tradelines, verified data points, dates, or material deficiencies relied upon, while presenting the systems output as a factual basis for denial despite its inaccuracy. clusters.name: Auto Lending — Principal, Principal Payments, Charged — Managing the loan or lease
sample_narratives:
Complaint Against Bank of America Deceptive Loan Servicing Practices I was in the process of purchasing an RV and was presented with several banks as potential lenders. One of those lenders was Bank of America. Before selecting any lender, I contacted Bank of America directly to confirm that they were a good fit and that they offered the specific loan servicing features I require. 

Prior to signing the loan documents, I explicitly discussed with Bank of America how the loan would be serviced, including how interest is calculated, how payments are applied, and the procedures for making principal-only payments and early payoff. I made it clear that the ability to make principal-only payments easily and frequently is essential to me, as I routinely make multiple payments per month and pay well above the minimum due. Bank of America confirmed that they could accommodate these needs. 

After the loan was originated and I attempted to make my first payment, I discovered that Bank of America does not allow principal-only payments to be made online. This directly contradicts what I was told prior to agreeing to the loan and was a deciding factor in my choice of lender. 

Instead, Bank of America requires customers to either : Mail in a physical payment with a written letter requesting that it be applied to principal only, hoping it is processed correctly, or Call in and navigate a lengthy automated system that does not allow for principal-only payments, then hope to reach a representative in the correct department who can manually process the payment. 

This process is unreasonable, inefficient, and risky. It forces customers to repeatedly disclose sensitive personal and account information over the phone and relies on manual handling that increases the likelihood of errors. More importantly, this structure actively discourages principal payments and appears designed to maximize interest collection rather than allow borrowers to reduce their balances efficiently. 

I belie

I attempted to make a payment on truist banks phone automated system, and it did not properly record the payment. Truist bank then charged me a fee of XXXX $ for " refunding a payment '' that did not go through. Talking with them yielded no results. A proper payment was made the day after but I am still being charged XXXX $

I have tried probably about 10 times in the last year to make principal payments or get a payoff amount for my auto loan and they do no provide the information or ability to do so. They hide this information and don't allow customers the ability to make principal payments so they can extract maximum interest charges. On their website there is NO information about your loan, interest rate, payoff balance, etc. and NO way to select principal payment in the payment system. On their app, it doesn't work most of the time and goes to an error screen, but one the rare occasion it opens the app there is still no way to find critical loan information or make principal payments. When I callied on the phone, twice it went through a automated system with no option to hear the critical loan information or make principal payments, and when you finally get directed to an " operator '' it stays on hold then disconnects. Even to get the information needed to refinance to another lender is not available on the site, app, or phone system.

To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to formally dispute the inaccurate and improperly reported bankruptcy information appearing on the credit file of XXXX XXXX. This letter serves as a notice of dispute, a demand for strict compliance with federal law, and a request for immediate corrective action. 
Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically 15 U.S.C. 1681b ( a ) ( 2 ), a consumer reporting agency may only furnish or report information when there is a lawful and verifiable permissible purpose. You are hereby placed on notice that the bankruptcy information you are reporting lacks demonstrated permissible purpose and has not been properly verified as required by law.

Further, under 15 U.S.C. 1681q, any person who knowingly and willfully obtains or furnishes consumer information under false pretenses is subject to federal liability. Continued reporting of unverified or inaccurately attributed bankruptcy data may constitute reporting under false pretenses if proper authorization and verification can not be produced.

Additionally, pursuant to 18 U.S.C. 1028A, strict identity verification is required where information may be the result of misidentification or identity-related error. I dispute that this bankruptcy has been accurately matched to me, and I demand proof that the information reported has been verified through reliable and lawful means.

Under 15 U.S.C. 1681c-2, I am exercising my right to block the reporting of inaccurate information resulting from identity error or misattribution. You are required to block and remove this bankruptcy entry unless and until you obtain direct, documentary verification from the courthouse of record, confirming : My full legal name, My Social Security number ( full or last XXXX digits ), My date of birth, and A certified match confirming I am the subject of the alleged bankruptcy.
